codeReview.summary: The fix commit directly addresses both prior blocking findings from keyboard-shortcuts: input guarding now executes before Cmd+K (with Escape explicitly exempted), and Escape no longer early-returns before clearing mixed UI state. Added hook-level integration tests cover the previously failing paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation-worker skill requires agent-browser for UI behavior changes, but this fix session appears to have completed without agent-browser/manual interaction evidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md ('Required Skills' says agent-browser MUST be invoked for UI changes); handoff 2026-04-17T02-13-39-497Z__fix-keyboard-shortcuts-scrutiny__bf4aee69-9cee-472f-92cb-391cab9be886.json includes only commandsRun and no interactiveChecks; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId bf4aee69-9cee-472f-92cb-391cab9be886 shows no agent-browser invocation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker spent debugging time on JSDOM platform behavior for modifier keys (Cmd vs Ctrl) in keyboard shortcut tests; this environment-specific testing quirk is not documented in shared library guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId bf4aee69-9cee-472f-92cb-391cab9be886 logs 'navigator.platform' as empty and adjusts tests from metaKey to ctrlKey; .factory/library/user-testing.md has no note about this keyboard-testing nuance.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the prior failed keyboard-shortcuts commit and this fix commit; the two previously reported blocking issues in useKeyboardShortcuts.ts are resolved and covered by targeted integration tests.
